---
title: "Applied Statistics I"
subtitle: "Pre‑Class Survey Summary"
author: "Math 260"
format:
  revealjs:
    chalkboard: true
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    toc: false
    toc-depth: 1
    reveal-options:
      slideNumber: true
    html-math-method: mathjax
    incremental: false
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    notes: true
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-color: "#EA6820"
from: markdown+emoji
execute:
  echo: true
  cache: true
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---


## Why you’re here

*   Required for pre‑health, STEM, business, or research paths
*   Many of you want to get deeper statistics understanding


## Common worries

*   “I’m not a math person” or it’s been a while since your last math class
*   The class moving too fast or falling behind
*   Applying concepts and learning R
*   Balancing coursework with work, athletics, and family responsibilities


## What Helps You Succeed


*   Step‑by‑step examples
*   Time in class to practice and ask questions
*   Clear expectations and low‑stakes chances to learn
*   Understanding ideas rather than memorizing formulas

## Our Approach

*   Prioritize understanding over speed
*   New ideas will come with **worked examples and practice**
*   Acknowledge struggle is normal in statistics.
*   Support is built in: class time, office hours, peers, MPC
